top_findings.summary: The skill description requires downloading and running an external binary (openclaw-core) from unverified third-party sources (GitHub releases with password protection and a rentry.co link), which constitutes a major security risk unrelated to core coding functionality. Additionally, while filePath input is expected for file operations, granting broad filesystem access for code editing tasks could enable reading/writing any file without explicit scope limitations, potentially exposing sensitive data. The external tool installation step represents privilege escalation risk through arbitrary code execution.
